title: tao-coherence: derive identity surface from single persona seed
description:
The hard lesson from anti-detect: any single datapoint is easy; the joint distribution kills you. Coherence must derive every observable from one persona seed consistently.

Vertical slice v0:
1. Hardware profile → screen resolution, devicePixelRatio, colorDepth
2. GPU → WebGL vendor/renderer string, canvas behavior
3. Locale → timezone, language, geolocation alignment
4. Network → proxy location matches timezone/locale
5. Validate with CreepJS self-hosted target — zero consistency failures

This is the 'forged papers' layer. Without it, perfect behavior dies on a mismatched GPU string.

See tao/SPEC.md §2.5 COHERENCE for full architecture.

title: tao-mirror: expand integrity probe with rebrowser CDP leak checks
description:
Current Tier-0 integrity probe covers navigator.webdriver, headless UA, plugins, languages, window.chrome. Missing CDP-specific tells that rebrowser-bot-detector catches.

Gaps to close:
1. Runtime.enable console signature (CDP injects a detectable console method)
2. Page.addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument ordering (automation scripts load before page scripts)
3. Dummy function leaks (ChromeDriver exposes internal communication functions)
4. Exposed CDP port / ws endpoint detection

Work:
1. Study rebrowser-bot-detector source for complete leak surface
2. Add each check to detect/probe.ts
3. Run probe against LiveActuator-driven real Chrome
4. Wire results into MIRROR combine() so Tier-0 failures cap the honest score

See rebrowser-bot-detector repo (public, self-hostable in CI).

title: tao-motor-v2: close the FP-Agent gaps (drive human-vs-TAO AUC toward 0.5)
description: Concrete, MEASURED gradient from the oracle-validated FP-Agent eval (human-vs-TAO AUC currently 0.999). Close in priority order, re-running eval/human-vs-tao.ts after each: (1) mousemove SAMPLE DENSITY — TAO emits ~36 moves/session vs human ~618 (16x too sparse); emit far more intermediate points at realistic sampling. (2) KEY-DWELL — hold latency idx39-42 is exactly 0 vs human ~85-105ms; add per-key press duration (keydown->keyup gap) to the motor. (3) CURVATURE VARIANCE — paths too smooth/uniform (idx5/7/11 direction-stdev, angle-range, curvature-distance); add micro-jitter/sub-movements so direction+curvature distributions widen. (4) CHUNKED SCROLL — single uniform burst (idx26/27 ~444 vs human ~180-197); break scrolling into smaller varied increments with pauses (and emit real sc/se offset events). (5) dangling key flags (idx43/44) + realistic typo count (idx45, human ~7.5 vs TAO ~0.9). Target: AUC well below the 7 agents, ideally toward 0.5.
design: Each fix is independently measurable via the validated featurizer. This is a real optimization loop now — change motor, re-run, watch AUC. No more self-grading.
